blast_radius.files_potentially_regenerated: any committed __snapshots__/*.snap that pin index.json or semantic-registry.json contents (count unknown until grep)
downstream_consumers: Local RAG vector index (.coderef/coderef-vectors.json or wherever rag-index writes)
RAG server running on port 52849 (started during fast-start session; may have died — verify before reindex)
validation_targets: Full vitest suite: 1274 pass / 66 fail expected (no new regressions vs pre-validation baseline)
RAG vector store freshness: file timestamp post-merge (post 2026-05-01T17:00 UTC)
Three analysis skills: each produces a report without throwing

risks.title: Test suite reveals a regression that was masked in the focused 26-test run
risks.mitigation: If full suite shows MORE failures than the post-merge baseline (66), halt validation and author a fix workorder. Do not snapshot-regen against a regression.

risks.title: RAG server is dead from the cascade failure earlier in the session
risks.mitigation: Check /coderef-rag-server status first. If server is down, restart it via /coderef-rag-server start before attempting reindex. The session received task failure notifications for the watcher, monitoring API, and Lloyd routers — RAG server status not confirmed.

risks.title: Analysis skills throw on the new richer relationship data
risks.mitigation: Spot-check is exactly the test for this. Read-only invocation; if a skill throws, capture the error and stop. Do not patch inline; surface as a follow-up workorder.

risks.title: Snapshot regen masks real regressions
risks.mitigation: Snapshot regen runs ONLY after the user explicitly confirms the diffs are intentional. Do not auto-update snapshots as part of the test run; review each diff first.

risks.title: RAG re-index takes a long time and stalls the workorder
risks.mitigation: Run reindex in background mode with logging. The workorder hard-stops after reindex completes; user can intervene if it hangs.

candidates.action: GREP for index/registry snapshots; regenerate selectively

decision_records.decision: Read-only validation; no code changes inside this workorder
decision_records.rationale: Goal is verification of consumer behavior post-merge. Mixing fixes into a validation pass muddies success criteria. If regressions surface, separate fix workorder.

decision_records.decision: Hard stops after RAG reindex and at final review
decision_records.rationale: Reindex is the only data-changing step (vector store rewrite). User confirms before snapshot regen, which is the only file-modifying step in the suite. Final hard stop is for closure approval.

decision_records.decision: Three specific analysis skills, in named order: analyze-coderef-semantics → coderef-analyze → data-source-report-use-case-analyzer
decision_records.rationale: Order chosen by deep-dive: first is most directly affected by header writing being real, second exercises aggregate behavior across skills, third is the heaviest semantic-registry consumer. Sequential execution surfaces problems early.

decision_records.decision: OPENCLAW → automation rename explicitly out of scope
decision_records.rationale: User instruction: address only coderef-core in this workorder; circle back to OPENCLAW after dispatch.
